data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Aesthetically pleasing designs are perceived as easier to use.
data.technical_spec: Minimalistic design with ample white space and intuitive layout.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Increased number of choices will increase the decision time.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Important buttons and links are large and placed strategically.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, preferring familiarity.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Average person can only keep 7 (plus or minus 2) items in working memory.
data.technical_spec: Simplified navigation and concise information displays.
